key_insight: A browser-accessible LLM was used to discover explicit graph constructions that raise lower bounds for the degree/diameter problem at $(\Delta,D)=(12,5)$ and $(16,5)$.
engineering_relevance: This shows that general-purpose LLMs can contribute to combinatorial theorem discovery even without a custom search agent or formal proof environment.
method: Interact with ChatGPT through the standard web interface
Iteratively develop an explicit graph construction from the conversation
Validate the result by proving improved lower bounds for $N(12,5)$ and $N(16,5)$
evidence_signal: The paper reports explicit constructions proving $N(12,5)\ge 34{,}992$ and $N(16,5)\ge 147{,}456$, improving prior recorded bounds.
watch_item: The discovery process is based on a visible transcript and manual interaction, so the approach may be hard to reproduce or automate at scale.
paper_title: New lower bounds for the degree/diameter problem via interaction with a browser-accessible LLM
